old_claim.note: Mobile wallets need card networks, merchant payment acceptance, and digital payment processing; blockchain is not a prerequisite for this scoped node.

new_claim.note: Mobile wallets scale through existing card/payment networks, issuer provisioning, merchant acceptance, and payment-processing partners; blockchain is not a prerequisite for this scoped node.

source_shape.source_locator: CFPB report on mobile-device operating systems and tap-to-pay practices describes wallet integration with payment cards, issuers, networks, and contactless merchant acceptance.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Phone payment wallets operate through existing payment-card and contactless-payment ecosystems rather than independent blockchain rails.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports keeping payment gateways as a commercial/scaling dependency: it documents the payment ecosystem around wallets without making gateways a hard engineering prerequisite for all wallet software.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: digital_payment_wallets -> digital_payment_gateways remains commercial_or_scaling_dependency; changed: evidence is now source-backed.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to cryptocurrency wallets rather than card/payment-network mobile wallets.
A stronger source showed the scoped 2011 mobile wallet could operate commercially without payment-network, issuer, or merchant-acceptance infrastructure.
short_reason: Payment rails are commercial scaling infrastructure, not blockchain.
